2. Tuck your chin and draw your knees toward your chest while reaching forward with your arms.
3. Roll your torso forward along your spine until you come up to a seated or prone position.
4. Reverse the motion in a controlled way to return to the supine start.
column19: Maintain a soft bend in the elbows and squeeze the shoulder blades together

column17: Enhances spine mobility and full body coordination
column18:
1. Lie face down with your legs straight and toes pointed.
2. Bend your knees to curl your heels toward your glutes while squeezing your hamstrings.
3. Hold the top briefly with control.
4. Slowly lower your feet back to the starting position.
column19: Engage the core and articulate the spine slowly while breathing evenly

column17: Strengthens hamstrings and improves knee flexion control
column18:
1. Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
2. Extend your arms out to the sides for stability.
3. Slowly lower both knees together to one side until you feel a gentle rotation in your hips.
4. Return your knees to center and repeat the movement to the other side.
column19: Press your hips into the bench and curl the heel with controlled motion

column17: Strengthens gluteal muscles and supports functional hip extension
column18:
1. Sit tall on a chair with feet hip-width apart and flat on the floor.
2. Press through the balls of your feet and lift your heels as high as possible.
3. Pause at the top, then lower your heels slowly back to the floor and repeat.
column19: Hinge from the hip and keep the torso tall while using the support

column17: Improves hip mobility and rotational range of motion
column18:
1. Stand tall with feet hip-width apart and gaze forward.
2. Shift your weight onto one foot and lift the opposite foot off the floor by bending the knee.
3. Keep the standing knee soft and your hips level while maintaining balance with eyes open.
4. Hold the position, then lower your foot and switch to the other leg.
column19: Anchor your pelvis and rotate the knees slowly with steady breathing

column17: Strengthens calf muscles and enhances ankle plantarflexion control
column18:
1. Sit on a chair with feet flat and a towel spread under your toes.
2. Press your heels into the floor and curl your toes to scrunch the towel toward you.
3. Keep your heels planted and extend your toes to push the towel back out.
4. Repeat the scrunch-and-release motions with steady control.
column19: Press through the balls of the feet and lift the heels with control

column17: Improves foot intrinsic strength and supports arch stability
column18:
1. Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
2. Extend both arms straight up toward the ceiling with palms facing each other.
3. Slowly lower one arm overhead toward the floor while keeping your ribs and hips still.
4. Return the arm to center and repeat with the other arm in a controlled rhythm.
column19: Anchor the heel and curl the toes deliberately to pull the towel

column17: Improves dynamic balance and gait coordination
column18:
1. Come to hands and knees with wrists under shoulders and knees under hips.
2. Drop your belly, lift your chest and tailbone to arch the spine into cow.
3. Round your back, tuck your chin and tailbone to create a cat position; flow between both.
column19: Take slow heel-to-toe steps and maintain an upright posture

column17: Enhances deep core activation and trunk stability
column18:
1. Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
2. Tuck your chin gently toward your chest to lengthen the back of the neck.
3. Lift your head a few inches off the ground while keeping the chin tucked.
4. Lower your head slowly back down with control.
column19: Press the low back into the floor and move the arms slowly with control

column17: Promotes spinal mobility and gentle trunk flexibility
column18:
1. Start on hands and knees with wrists under shoulders and knees under hips.
2. Reach one arm underneath the opposite arm and slide the shoulder and ear toward the floor.
3. Rotate your torso to open the chest while keeping hips facing the floor.
4. Return the arm to the starting position and repeat on the other side.
column19: Move through full spinal flexion and extension with deep, steady breaths

column17: Improves neck posture and strengthens neck stabilizing muscles
column18:
1. Lie on your side with the bottom shoulder stacked and the bottom elbow bent to 90 degrees in front of you.
2. Use your top hand to gently press the bottom forearm down toward the floor until you feel a mild stretch in the back of the shoulder.
3. Keep hips and knees stacked and relax into the stretch, then release and switch sides.
column19: Tuck your chin and lift the head while maintaining a neutral spine

column17: Enhances thoracic rotation and shoulder mobility
column18:
1. Stand beside a waist-high table with feet hip-width apart.
2. Place your nearest hand flat on the table at hip level.
3. Shift weight into the hand and slide it slowly sideways along the table while keeping your torso upright.
4. Reverse the slide back to the start and repeat controlled.
column19: Rotate through the thoracic spine slowly while keeping the low back stable

column17: Improves shoulder internal rotation and flexibility
column18:
1. Stand with your back and head against a wall and feet a few inches away.
2. Bend your elbows slightly and let your arms rest by your sides or in a goalpost position.
3. Draw your shoulder blades down and together without shrugging or bending your elbows.
4. Hold the squeeze briefly then relax and repeat.
column19: Anchor the shoulder blade and rotate the arm with controlled motion

column17: Promotes lateral shoulder mobility and controlled scapular movement
column18:
1. Stand perpendicular to a wall and place your palm and inner forearm flat on the wall at shoulder height.
2. Keep the arm straight and slowly rotate your chest away from the wall.
3. Stop when you feel a gentle stretch in the front of your upper arm.
4. Hold briefly then return to the starting position and repeat on the other side.
column19: Slide the arm laterally with the elbow close and avoid shrugging the shoulder

column17: Enhances scapular control and supports upright posture
column18:
1. Sit with your forearm supported on a flat surface and your hand hanging off the edge, palm neutral.
2. Keep the forearm still and move the wrist slowly toward the thumb side (radial deviation).
3. Reverse the motion and move the wrist toward the little-finger side (ulnar deviation), then repeat.
column19: Press your shoulder blades gently into the wall while keeping your ribs down

column17: Improves wrist mobility and range of motion
column18:
1. Lie face down with your forehead on folded hands and arms extended overhead in a Y, thumbs pointing up.
2. Lift both arms a few inches off the floor by squeezing the shoulder blades together and keeping the thumbs up.
3. Lower the arms slowly to the floor and repeat.
column19: Move the wrist slowly through a comfortable range while keeping the forearm supported

column17: Strengthens upper back and shoulder stabilizers
column18:
1. Stand in front of a chair with feet hip-width apart and toes slightly turned out.
2. Bend at the hips and knees to lower into a shallow squat while keeping weight over the heels.
3. Lightly touch the seat with your butt or hover just above it without fully sitting down.
4. Press through the heels to stand tall and repeat.
column19: Hinge at the hips, keep the neck neutral, and lift using your shoulder blades

column17: Promotes cardiovascular activity and improves balance and coordination
column18:
1. Lie on your side with hips stacked and knees bent at about 45 degrees.
2. Rest your head on your arm and bring your heels together.
3. Keeping feet touching, lift the top knee toward the ceiling by opening the hip.
4. Lower the knee with control and repeat.
column19: Stand tall, engage your core, and march with controlled breaths

column17: Strengthens hip external rotators and glute muscles for improved lateral stability
column18:
1. Sit tall on the floor with the soles of your feet together and knees dropping outward.
2. Hold your feet or ankles and draw your heels toward your groin.
3. Press your knees gently toward the floor using your elbows.
4. Lean forward slightly from the hips while keeping a straight back.
column19: Keep hips stacked and core braced as you open the knee

column17: Challenges static balance and enhances postural control in narrow stance
column18:
1. Stand tall beside a stable support and place one hand lightly on it for balance.
2. Shift your weight onto the standing leg and engage your core.
3. Lift the outside leg straight out to the side to hip height with toes pointing forward.
4. Lower the leg slowly with control and repeat, then switch sides.
column19: Keep hips level, soften the knees, and focus your gaze forward

column17: Promotes calf flexibility and ankle dorsiflexion for improved walking mechanics
column18:
1. Stand with feet hip-width apart and knees slightly bent.
2. Draw your belly button toward your spine and tuck your tailbone to flatten the lower back.
3. Hold the tilt for 2–3 seconds while breathing normally.
4. Release to a neutral spine and repeat.
column19: Press the back heel down and keep the back leg straight for a steady stretch

column17: Activates deep abdominal muscles to enhance pelvic alignment and core stability
column18:
1. Sit or stand tall with shoulders relaxed.
2. Tilt your right ear toward your right shoulder using only your neck muscles.
3. Apply gentle pressure with your right hand if you need more range.
4. Return to center and repeat on the left side.
column19: Tuck the pelvis by engaging the core and avoid overarching the lower back

column17: Encourages gentle spinal elongation and relaxation while stretching the hips and back
column18:
1. Sit on a chair with feet flat and hands behind your head.
2. Keep your hips facing forward and rotate your chest to the right using your thoracic spine.
3. Pause at the end range while maintaining an upright posture.
4. Return to center and repeat to the left.
column19: Reach the hips back toward the heels and breathe while maintaining a long spine

column17: Enhances thoracic mobility and rotational range of motion
column18:
1. Stand tall with feet hip-width apart and arms extended to the sides at shoulder height.
2. Keep a soft bend in the elbows and make small controlled circles forward using only your shoulders.
3. Continue for the chosen count then reverse the direction.
4. Keep your core engaged and breathe steadily.
column19: Sit tall and rotate from the mid back with control

column17: Improves shoulder elevation strength and upper trapezius engagement
column18:
1. Stand sideways next to a wall with the elbow bent at 90° and the upper arm at your side.
2. Place your palm or forearm against the wall and press inward without moving the elbow.
3. Hold the press for 10–20 seconds while maintaining steady tension.
4. Relax and repeat on the other side if needed.
column19: Lift straight up and lower slowly without shrugging the neck

column17: Builds internal shoulder stability and rotator cuff endurance
column18:
1. Kneel and make fists, placing your knuckles flat on the floor under your shoulders.
2. Lift your torso so your body forms a straight line from shoulders to knees.
3. Brace your core and squeeze your glutes while breathing evenly.
4. Hold for the chosen duration then lower your knees to rest.
column19: Keep elbow pinned to your side and press inward without shrugging

column17: Builds core strength and promotes shoulder and spinal stability
column18:
1. Stand with your back against a wall and feet hip-width apart.
2. Place your arms against the wall in a "V" at about 30–45 degrees with elbows bent.
3. Slowly slide your arms upward along the wall until they are overhead or as high as you can while keeping contact.
4. Pause, then slowly slide your arms back down to the starting position.
column19: Maintain a straight line from head to knees and brace the core

column17: Improves scapular control and functional shoulder elevation
column18:
1. Sit upright on a chair with feet flat and knees at about 90 degrees.
2. Press your heel into the floor and tighten the quadriceps to attempt to straighten the knee a few degrees without lifting the foot.
3. Hold the slight extension for a short count while keeping the thigh engaged.
4. Slowly relax the muscle and return to the starting relaxed position.
column19: Keep forearms against the wall and slide in the scapular plane without shrugging

column17: Increases low-intensity cardiovascular activity and lateral movement coordination
column18:
1. Stand with your back against a wall and feet hip-width apart a short distance from the wall.
2. Slide your back down the wall until your knees form a high-angle bend (less than a deep squat) with hips and shoulders touching the wall.
3. Hold the position with core engaged and weight through your heels.
4. Press through your heels and slide up the wall to return to standing.
column19: Step with control, keep chest up and avoid letting the knees collapse inward

column17: Strengthens quadriceps and improves knee joint control
column18:
1. Stand tall near a wall or chair for balance.
2. Shift weight onto the supporting leg and lift the other leg slightly to the side with the knee straight.
3. Press the lifted leg outward into your hand or imagined resistance without letting the leg move.
4. Hold the press while keeping hips level and breathing steadily.
column19: Keep the thigh stable and extend the knee with slow controlled motion

column17: Enhances lateral hip strength and pelvic stability
column18:
1. Sit upright on a chair with feet flat and knees at 90 degrees.
2. Lift one forefoot to tap the floor a short distance forward, then return that foot to flat.
3. Alternate feet in a steady rhythm while keeping your torso still.
column19: Keep hips level and abduct the leg without leaning your torso

column17: Promotes ankle mobility and lower limb coordination
column18:
1. Sit with feet flat and knees at 90 degrees.
2. Lift your toes while keeping your heels pressed down and spread your toes as wide as possible.
3. Hold the spread briefly, then relax your toes and repeat.
column19: Brace your core and tap the foot deliberately without bouncing

column17: Develops single leg balance and proprioceptive awareness
column18:
1. Stand tall with one hand lightly on a wall and place one foot directly in front of the other heel-to-toe.
2. Shift weight onto the front foot in a controlled manner while keeping your gaze forward and the supporting hand on the wall.
3. Bring the back foot forward to meet the front foot while maintaining upright posture.
4. Repeat heel-to-toe steps, alternating the leading foot each time.
column19: Keep the standing knee slightly bent and control each touch to the wall

column17: Strengthens intrinsic foot muscles and improves toe dexterity
column18:
1. Begin on hands and knees with hands under shoulders and knees under hips.
2. Slide your right arm straight forward along the floor while sliding your left leg straight back, keeping hips level.
3. Hold the extended position briefly with core engaged.
4. Slide both limbs back to the start and repeat on the opposite side.
column19: Keep the heel grounded and actively spread the toes while maintaining a neutral ankle

column17: Improves cervical posture and head alignment
column18:
1. Stand beside a stable surface and place one hand lightly on it for balance.
2. Shift weight onto the supporting leg and hinge slightly at the hip.
3. Swing the free leg forward and then backward in a controlled pendulum motion while keeping your torso still.
column19: Retract chin and glide the head straight back while keeping shoulders relaxed

column17: Enhances thoracic spine mobility and rotational range
column18:
1. Get on hands and knees in a tabletop position with shoulders over wrists and hips over knees.
2. Brace your core and keep your spine neutral.
3. Slide the right knee out to the side along the floor while keeping the foot in contact, then slide it back.
4. Repeat for desired reps and then switch to the left leg.
column19: Hinge at the hips and rotate your chest while keeping the pelvis square

column17: Strengthens core stability and improves trunk endurance
column18:
1. Stand tall with feet hip-width apart and a slight bend in the knees.
2. Step the right foot out to the side while keeping your weight centered.
3. Bring the left foot to meet the right with a controlled movement and maintain a soft knee bend.
4. Continue stepping side to side at a steady, low-impact pace.
column19: Maintain controlled movement and proper alignment throughout

column17: Improves lateral mobility and supports balance with low joint stress
column18:
1. Lie on your back with legs extended and toes relaxed.
2. Tighten one thigh muscle and press the back of that knee gently toward the floor.
3. Hold the contraction for 5–10 seconds, relax, and repeat on the other leg.
column19: Take controlled side steps with knees slightly bent and weight centered over your feet

column17: Strengthens gluteal muscles and promotes hip extension control
column18:
1. Sit with one leg in front and the other to the side, both knees bent at 90 degrees.
2. Square your hips and keep your torso upright.
3. Lean forward over the front shin to deepen the front hip stretch.
4. Rotate your torso and lean over the back leg to mobilize the rear hip, then switch sides.
column19: Maintain controlled movement and proper alignment throughout

column17: Builds hip abductor strength and improves pelvic stability
column18:
1. Lie on your back and lift one foot off the ground.
2. Point the toes and slowly trace large circles with your ankle in a controlled motion.
3. Reverse the direction of the circles and repeat with the other ankle.
column19: Keep hips stacked and avoid rotating the torso as you lift the top leg

column17: Promotes ankle mobility and enhances proprioceptive awareness
column18:
1. Sit and cross one foot over the opposite knee.
2. Grasp the toes and gently pull them back toward your shin until you feel a stretch along the arch.
3. Hold briefly, release, and repeat on the other foot.
column19: Move only the foot from the ankle joint and control the motion throughout

column17: Improves single leg stability and neuromuscular balance control
column18:
1. Stand upright with feet together and eyes focused ahead.
2. Place the heel of one foot directly in front of the toes of the other on an imaginary line.
3. Walk forward slowly, placing each foot heel-to-toe and keeping steady balance.
4. Keep arms relaxed at your sides or extended for balance and continue with controlled steps.
column19: Keep a soft bend in the standing knee and maintain an upright posture

column17: Improves plantar fascia flexibility and supports foot mobility
column18:
1. Lie on your back with one knee bent and the other leg extended straight.
2. Place your hands lightly behind your head or across your chest and keep your neck neutral.
3. Exhale and lift only your head, neck, and shoulder blades a few inches off the floor.
4. Inhale and slowly lower back down with control.
column19: Pull the toes toward the shin and keep the foot aligned with a relaxed ankle

column17: Enhances gait coordination and challenges dynamic balance while walking
column18:
1. Kneel with big toes touching and knees spread comfortably apart.
2. Fold forward and lower your torso onto your thighs while stretching your arms forward or alongside your body.
3. Rest your forehead or chest on the floor and remain in the position while relaxing your muscles.
column19: Take small controlled steps and place each foot heel to toe while staying upright

column17: Improves upper trapezius flexibility and neck range of motion
column18:
1. Lie face down on a mat with forehead resting and arms by your sides, palms down.
2. Keep your neck neutral and squeeze your shoulder blades together without shrugging.
3. Lift only your chest slightly by letting the shoulder blades retract, keeping arms relaxed.
4. Hold briefly and then relax back to the start.
column19: Keep shoulder down and spine long while tilting the head toward the opposite shoulder

column17: Enhances thoracic mobility and spinal rotation control
column18:
1. Stand side-on to a wall and place your closest hand on the wall at shoulder height.
2. Step the far foot away from the wall and push your hips out so your body forms a diagonal.
3. Move the hand laterally along the wall while stepping the feet sideways to follow.
4. Continue walking sideways for the desired distance, then reverse to return.
column19: Keep hips stable and rotate through the thoracic spine with control

column17: Strengthens scapular stabilizers and improves posterior shoulder alignment
column18:
1. Stand tall with feet hip-width and arms by your sides, thumbs pointing forward.
2. Raise both arms in the scapular plane about 30–45° from your body up to shoulder height.
3. Lower your arms slowly back to the start with control.
column19: Squeeze the shoulder blades together without shrugging the shoulders

column17: Builds shoulder mobility and active upper extremity coordination
column18:
1. Hold a rolled towel lengthwise in one hand.
2. Squeeze the towel as hard as possible with your fingers and thumb.
3. Hold the squeeze for 5–10 seconds.
4. Release slowly and repeat before switching hands.
column19: Maintain controlled movement and proper alignment throughout

column17: Improves hand grip strength and forearm muscular endurance
column18:
1. Lie prone with legs extended and forehead on the floor.
2. Place your arms alongside your torso with palms facing down.
3. Lift your chest and arms a few inches off the floor while squeezing your shoulder blades.
4. Hold for 5–10 seconds, then lower and repeat.
column19: Grip the towel firmly using fingers and thumb without tensing the forearm excessively

column17: Develops chest and triceps activation with reduced load on joints
column18:
1. Stand with feet together and arms relaxed at waist height.
2. Step your right foot to the right about shoulder-width and bend your knees slightly.
3. Bring your left foot to meet your right and tap the floor while moving your arms low to the right.
4. Repeat the same motion to the left side, alternating rhythmically.
column19: Maintain a neutral spine and lower with controlled elbows close to the body

column17: Improves lateral agility and lower body coordination
column18:
1. Sit on the edge of a firm chair with feet flat and knees bent.
2. Straighten one knee a few inches by contracting the front thigh, keeping a small bend.
3. Hold the lifted position briefly, then lower the foot slowly.
4. Repeat for desired reps, then switch legs.
column19: Step laterally with controlled weight transfer and keep the chest lifted

column17: Activates quadriceps to improve knee extension control
column18:
1. Stand tall with feet hip-width apart and hands on hips.
2. Step one foot back into a long lunge while keeping the front knee over the ankle.
3. Tilt your pelvis forward and shift hips until you feel a stretch in the front of the back hip.
4. Keep your torso upright, hold the stretch, then switch legs.
column19: Support the thigh and contract the quad to extend the knee with controlled movement

column17: Enhances hip flexor endurance and promotes circulation in lower limbs
column18:
1. Sit upright on a chair with feet flat and knees at about 90 degrees.
2. Press through the balls of both feet to lift your heels as high as possible.
3. Pause at the top, then lower your heels slowly back to the floor.
4. Repeat for desired repetitions.
column19: Sit tall with a neutral spine and lift each knee slowly without leaning back

column17: Builds calf muscle strength and supports ankle stability
column18:
1. Sit on a chair with bare feet flat and a towel laid on the floor under your toes.
2. Extend your toes forward and then curl them to hook the towel edge.
3. Pull the towel toward you by flexing your toes while keeping heels down.
4. Release the towel and repeat the curl-and-pull motion in control.
column19: Press through the balls of the feet and lift the heels with controlled ankle alignment

column17: Develops lower body strength and functional sit to stand ability
column18:
1. Sit upright on a chair with feet flat and hands on your hips.
2. Tuck your tailbone and draw your belly button toward your spine to flatten the lower back.
3. Release the tuck and arch your lower back slightly while lifting the chest.
4. Alternate the posterior and anterior tilts in a slow, controlled rhythm.
column19: Maintain controlled movement and proper alignment throughout

column17: Strengthens lateral core muscles and improves side stability
column18:
1. Sit or stand tall with shoulders relaxed and head facing forward.
2. Place your right hand gently against the right side of your head.
3. Press your head into your hand while resisting movement with your hand for a few seconds.
4. Switch sides and repeat the resisted press on the left side.
column19: Stack shoulders over elbow and lift hips into a straight line while breathing steadily

column17: Improves thoracic mobility and upright posture
column18:
1. Start on hands and knees with wrists under shoulders and knees under hips, keeping a neutral spine.
2. Brace your core and press your palms into the floor.
3. Slowly slide your hands forward while keeping hips and torso steady.
4. Pause at end range, then pull your hands back under your shoulders.
column19: Sit tall and extend through the thoracic spine while keeping the core braced

column17: Promotes scapular control and shoulder girdle stability
column18:
1. Lie on your side with knees bent and place a rolled towel under your upper arm near the elbow.
2. Tuck your elbow against your side and rest your forearm across your belly.
3. Rotate your forearm upward toward the ceiling while keeping the elbow fixed to your side.
4. Lower your forearm back down with control.
column19: Keep shoulders down and back and avoid shrugging toward the ears

column17: Enhances scapular mobility and shoulder range of motion
column18:
1. Sit or stand with elbow bent 90° at your side and make a fist with the thumb pointing up.
2. Keep the elbow stationary and rotate your forearm so the palm faces up.
3. Rotate back to the starting hammer-grip position with control.
column19: Maintain a neutral pelvis and slide the shoulders forward with slow controlled motion

column17: Strengthens shoulder external rotators and improves rotational control
column18:
1. Place forearms on the mat shoulder-width apart and set knees on the floor under hips.
2. Engage your core and keep a straight line from head to knees without sagging or arching.
3. Hold the position while breathing steadily for the desired time.
4. Lower hips gently to the mat to rest.
column19: Keep the elbow tucked to the side and rotate slowly while stabilizing the shoulder blade

column17: Builds core endurance and enhances trunk stability
column18:
1. Sit tall on a chair with feet flat and shoulders relaxed.
2. Draw your chin straight back toward the spine without tilting the head up or down.
3. Hold the tucked position for a few seconds while breathing evenly.
4. Release slowly and return to neutral.
column19: Keep a straight line from head to hips and brace the core

column17: Promotes cervical alignment and deep neck muscle activation
column18:
1. Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat, and place elbows bent at 90 degrees by your sides.
2. Press your lower back into the floor and keep elbows and wrists lightly touching the ground.
3. Slowly slide your arms outward and upward within a comfortable range while maintaining contact.
4. Return arms to the starting position with controlled motion.
column19: Gently retract the chin keeping eyes level and lengthen the back of the neck

column17: Enhances hip flexor activation and circulation through rhythmic seated movement
column18:
1. Sit upright on a chair with back straight and feet flat.
2. Extend your right leg until the knee is nearly straight and hold briefly.
3. Lower the right leg with control and immediately extend the left leg.
4. Continue alternating legs in a steady, controlled rhythm.
column19: Sit tall with hips back and lift knees alternately with controlled breath

column17: Improves quadriceps activation and knee joint mobility
column18:
1. Lie on your back with legs straight and toes pointing up.
2. Squeeze the front of one thigh and press the back of the knee gently into the floor.
3. Hold the contraction for a few seconds.
4. Relax and repeat on the other leg.
column19: Keep core braced and back against the chair while extending the knee slowly

column17: Enhances quadriceps engagement and improves knee stability
column18:
1. Stand tall with feet hip-width apart and hands on your hips.
2. Shift weight onto your left leg and keep it slightly bent.
3. Lift your right leg a few inches out to the side with toes pointing forward.
4. Lower the right leg with control and repeat, then switch sides.
column19: Squeeze the thigh and press the knee into the mat while keeping the pelvis level

column17: Strengthens hip abductor muscles and enhances lateral pelvic control
column18:
1. Sit tall on the edge of a chair and bring the soles of your feet together.
2. Hold your feet with your hands and let your knees fall open to the sides.
3. Keep your spine straight and gently press your elbows on your inner thighs.
4. Breathe calmly and maintain the position for several slow breaths.
column19: Stand tall with hips square and lift the leg without tilting the pelvis

column17: Improves inner thigh flexibility and hip range of motion
column18:
1. Sit tall on a chair with knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
2. Lift one foot and tap the toes to the floor in front of you, then return to the start.
3. Alternate feet at a controlled, steady pace.
column19: Maintain controlled movement and proper alignment throughout

column17: Engages lower abdominal muscles and promotes ankle mobility
column18:
1. Stand behind a sturdy chair and place both hands lightly on the backrest for support.
2. Shift weight onto one foot and bend the standing knee slightly.
3. Lift the other foot and place the toes beside the ankle or against the inner calf of the standing leg.
4. Hold your balance, then lower the raised foot and repeat on the other side.
column19: Keep the low back neutral and lower each toe with control while breathing evenly

column17: Challenges balance and improves single leg stability
column18:
1. Sit tall on the floor with one leg extended and the other knee bent.
2. Loop a strap around the ball of the extended foot and hold the ends with both hands.
3. Keep the knee straight and gently pull the strap to flex the foot toward you.
4. Hold the stretch while breathing evenly, then switch sides.
column19: Keep hips level and a soft support knee while lightly touching the chair for balance

column17: Increases calf flexibility and ankle dorsiflexion range
column18:
1. Stand with one foot in front of the other and set both feet wider than hip-width.
2. Plant both feet evenly and align your hips and shoulders forward.
3. Soften your knees slightly and engage your core to stabilize.
4. Keep your gaze forward and hold, then repeat with the opposite foot forward.
column19: Maintain controlled movement and proper alignment throughout

column17: Activates deep core muscles and promotes coordinated lower limb movement
column18:
1. Lie prone with your legs extended and forearms on the floor under your shoulders.
2. Press your forearms down and lift your chest into a gentle, low backbend.
3. Lengthen through your spine, relax your shoulders away from your ears, and breathe evenly.
column19: Maintain a neutral spine and brace the core as you lift each knee slowly

column17: Encourages gentle spinal extension and supports upright posture
column18:
1. Sit tall with feet flat and shoulders relaxed.
2. Interlace fingers and place palms on your forehead for gentle support.
3. Tuck your chin and nod your head forward slowly against the hands.
4. Return to neutral with control and repeat as needed.
column19: Maintain controlled movement and proper alignment throughout

column17: Enhances shoulder mobility and thoracic rotation
column18:
1. Stand with feet hip-width and hinge forward at the hips until your torso is nearly parallel to the floor.
2. Rest your non-working hand on your thigh for support.
3. Let the working arm hang relaxed and swing it in small controlled circles.
4. Reverse the circle direction after several rotations.
column19: Keep the top hip stacked and rotate the shoulder with slow control

column17: Promotes gentle shoulder joint mobility and fluid movement
column18:
1. Lie on your back with hips and knees bent to 90 degrees and toes lightly touching the floor.
2. Press your lower back gently into the mat and engage your core.
3. Slide one heel along the floor diagonally away from your body while keeping your hips stable.
4. Return the heel to the starting position and repeat on the other side.
column19: Maintain controlled movement and proper alignment throughout

column17: Improves scapular control and shoulder range of motion
column18:
1. Stand side-on to a wall with the elbow of the working arm bent at 90 degrees.
2. Place the outer forearm and elbow against the wall at shoulder height.
3. Press your elbow outward into the wall without moving your torso.
4. Maintain the press and breathe steadily while keeping the shoulder engaged.
column19: Brace the core and slide the hand diagonally without arching the lower back

column17: Builds shoulder stability and muscular endurance
column18:
1. Stand facing a wall and extend one arm with the palm up and fingers pointing toward the ceiling.
2. Place the palm and fingers flat on the wall at shoulder height.
3. Lean your body forward slowly until you feel a stretch along the inner forearm.
4. Keep the elbow straight and breathe steadily while holding the stretch.
column19: Press evenly into the wall keeping the shoulder blades down and avoid shrugging

column17: Strengthens core and lateral trunk stability
column18:
1. Stand facing a corner and place your forearms or palms on each wall at shoulder height.
2. Step one foot forward to create a staggered stance.
3. Lean your torso forward until you feel a stretch across the chest.
4. Keep your shoulders down and breathe steadily while holding the position.
column19: Lift the hips and maintain a straight line from knees to shoulders

column17: Opens chest and improves anterior shoulder flexibility
column18:
1. Lie face down on the floor with legs straight and arms extended overhead in a Y shape.
2. Keep your neck neutral and gaze toward the floor.
3. Squeeze your shoulder blades and lift your arms off the floor into the Y until the upper back engages.
4. Lower your arms with control until they lightly touch the floor and repeat.
column19: Maintain controlled movement and proper alignment throughout

column17: Enhances upper back strength and scapular stability
column18:
1. Stand tall with feet hip-width and knees soft.
2. Step your right foot to the right, then bring your left foot to meet it and tap lightly.
3. Continue stepping side to side at a steady pace and swing your arms naturally.
column19: Keep your gaze down and lift from the shoulder blades without overextending the neck

column17: Promotes knee and hip mobility through controlled flexion and extension
column18:
1. Lie on your side with hips and knees bent to about 90 degrees and feet stacked.
2. Keep your feet together and lift the top knee upward by opening the hip while keeping the pelvis stable.
3. Lower the knee with control and repeat, then switch sides.
column19: Slide the heel with control and keep the pelvis stable

column17: Strengthens hip abductors and enhances lateral pelvic stability
column18:
1. Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
2. Keep both shoulders down and slowly drop both knees to one side.
3. Pause while feeling the gentle rotation in your hips and lower back.
4. Bring knees back to center and repeat to the other side.
column19: Keep hips stacked and avoid rolling the pelvis backward

column17: Improves hip joint mobility and rotational range of motion
column18:
1. Sit upright on a chair with feet flat and knees bent 90 degrees.
2. Lift one foot slightly off the floor and turn the sole inward at the ankle.
3. Hold the inward position briefly while keeping the rest of the leg still.
4. Return the foot to neutral and repeat with the other ankle.
column19: Move slowly and keep the lower back gently pressed to the floor

column17: Targets ankle muscles to improve inversion control and proprioception
column18:
1. Stand tall with feet hip-width apart and hold a stable surface for balance.
2. Shift your weight back and lift your toes so your heels bear your weight.
3. Shift your weight forward and lift your heels so your toes bear your weight.
4. Alternate rocking between heels and toes with slow, controlled movements.
column19: Stabilize the shin and invert the foot with controlled movement

column17: Enhances ankle flexibility and balance during weight transfers
column18:
1. Sit or stand with bare feet flat on the floor and weight evenly distributed.
2. Spread your toes and press the ball of the foot into the floor.
3. Curl your toes downward to grip the floor and lift the arch slightly.
4. Hold the curl briefly, then relax and repeat.
column19: Shift weight slowly with soft knees and an upright torso

column17: Activates foot intrinsic muscles to support arch stability and toe control
column18:
1. Stand with one foot on an imaginary straight line and shoulders over hips.
2. Step forward placing the heel of the front foot directly in front of the toes of the back foot on the line.
3. Keep hips level and eyes forward while maintaining balance.
4. Continue walking heel-to-toe along the line and switch leading foot.
column19: Keep toes relaxed and curl from the ball of the foot with control

column17: Challenges dynamic balance and improves lateral foot placement accuracy
column18:
1. Start on hands and knees with wrists under shoulders and knees under hips.
2. Brace your core and extend one arm straight forward to shoulder height.
3. Keep hips and legs stationary and maintain a neutral spine.
4. Retract the arm under control and repeat on the other side.
column19: Take small controlled steps while keeping an upright posture

column17: Encourages spinal mobility and gentle movement coordination
column18:
1. Sit upright with feet flat and one hand placed behind your back.
2. Rotate your head about 45 degrees away from the side you want to stretch.
3. Tuck your chin and tilt your head down toward the opposite armpit.
4. Use the free hand to gently guide the head deeper into the stretch.
column19: Move the spine smoothly with steady breaths and soft knees

column17: Improves neck lateral mobility and muscle flexibility
column18:
1. Start on hands and knees with hands under shoulders and knees under hips.
2. Place one hand behind your head while keeping the other hand firmly planted.
3. Rotate your torso to lift the elbow of the hand behind your head toward the ceiling.
4. Rotate back to bring that elbow down and thread it under your torso toward the opposite hand.
column19: Keep spine tall and shoulder blade down

column17: Enhances thoracic spine rotation and core coordination
column18:
1. Sit or stand upright and extend one arm straight across your chest.
2. Reach the opposite hand to grasp the extended arm near the upper arm or elbow.
3. Pull the arm gently across your chest while keeping your shoulders relaxed.
column19: Keep hips level and rotate through the upper back slowly

column17: Builds shoulder stability and upward rotation control
column18:
1. Stand tall with feet hip-width apart and arms at your sides, thumbs pointing up.
2. Hinge slightly at the hips and lift both arms diagonally in the scapular plane to shoulder height.
3. Squeeze your shoulder blades together and pause briefly at the top.
4. Slowly lower your arms back to the starting position with control.
column19: Keep core braced and walk the hands up the wall with controlled motion

column17: Enhances wrist extension flexibility and forearm muscle length
column18:
1. Place your fists shoulder-width apart on the floor and extend your legs behind you into a straight line.
2. Engage your core and glutes to keep hips level and maintain a neutral neck.
3. Breathe evenly while holding a rigid plank position.
4. Lower your knees to the ground and push back to a resting position when finished.
column19: Keep the elbow extended and gently pull the wrist back until you feel a stretch

column17: Develops core endurance while maintaining wrist alignment
column18:
1. Stand with feet hip-width apart and hinge at the hips with a soft bend in the knees.
2. Let your arms hang toward the floor with palms facing each other and keep a neutral spine.
3. Open your arms out to the sides by squeezing your shoulder blades until your arms are roughly parallel to the ground.
4. Slowly return your arms to the starting position with control.
column19: Keep a straight line from head to heels and brace the core to prevent sagging

column17: Strengthens posterior shoulder muscles and improves scapular retraction
column18:
1. Stand facing a sturdy door and grasp the edge or handles at waist height.
2. Lean back with straight arms, bracing your core and angling your body.
3. Pull your elbows toward your ribs and squeeze your shoulder blades, holding the position.
4. Release slowly and step back to reset.
column19: Maintain controlled movement and proper alignment throughout

column17: Enhances mid back strength and scapular stabilization
column18:
1. Stand with feet hip-width apart, knees soft, and hips slightly bent.
2. Take a slow, controlled step to the side by pushing off the opposite foot.
3. Follow with the other foot and maintain low, steady movement.
column19: Stand tall, keep elbows close, and press back against the door with steady tension

column17: Strengthens quadriceps and improves knee extension control
column18:
1. Sit upright on a chair with your back straight and both feet flat on the floor.
2. Extend one leg until the knee is straight and toes point upward.
3. Hold the extended position, then lower slowly and repeat with the other leg.
column19: Sit tall and control the knee extension with a braced core

column17: Activates hamstrings to enhance posterior thigh stability
column18:
1. Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
2. Dig one heel into the floor and tighten the back of your thigh.
3. Hold the contraction while keeping your hips and lower back still.
4. Release the muscle and relax before repeating on the other side.
column19: Press the heel into the surface and keep the pelvis and low back stable

column17: Improves hip abductor strength and lateral balance
column18:
1. Stand beside a chair and place one hand lightly on the back for support.
2. Shift weight onto the standing leg and lift the other leg straight out to the side without leaning.
3. Keep toes pointing forward and the lifted leg straight but not locked.
4. Lower the leg slowly to the starting position and repeat on the other side.
column19: Stand tall and lift the leg to the side without leaning the torso

column17: Increases hip external rotation mobility and glute flexibility
column18:
1. Sit tall near the edge of a chair and place one ankle over the opposite thigh so the ankle rests above the knee.
2. Keep your back straight and gently press the raised knee downward with your hand.
3. Lean forward from your hips until you feel a gentle stretch in the hip and glute.
4. Hold briefly and then return upright before switching sides.
column19: Sit upright and hinge from the hips to keep the spine long during the stretch

column17: Strengthens ankle evertors to support foot alignment
column18:
1. Sit with knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
2. Place your hand against the outside edge of one foot near the little toes.
3. Push the outer edge of the foot outward into your hand without letting the foot move.
4. Hold the pressure with steady breathing while keeping the ankle stable.
column19: Keep the ankle neutral and press outward with steady controlled tension

column17: Enhances shoulder mobility and controlled reaching patterns
column18:
1. Stand tall with weight on one leg and the other foot hovering a few inches off the ground.
2. Imagine a small clock around the standing foot and keep your hips level.
3. Tap the hovering foot lightly to a nearby clock position with controlled motion.
4. Return to center and repeat for each chosen direction without shifting the standing foot.
column19: Move the leg with small controlled reaches while keeping the hips level and core engaged

column17: Improves intrinsic foot strength and fine motor control
column18:
1. Stand with one foot flat on a small towel on the floor.
2. Curl your toes to grab and scrunch the towel toward you using only your foot.
3. Relax your toes and spread them to smooth the towel back out.
column19: Stand with weight evenly distributed and use controlled toe flexion to scrunch the towel

column17: Builds lateral hip strength and improves pelvic stability
column18:
1. Lie on your side with the bottom leg slightly bent and your head supported by your bottom arm.
2. Stack your hips and shoulders and extend your top leg straight.
3. Lift the top leg upward to about hip level with toes pointing forward.
4. Lower the leg with control to the start position and repeat.
column19: Keep hips stacked and avoid rotating the torso as you lift the leg

column17: Enhances core stability and coordinated trunk control
column18:
1. Lie on your back with arms extended toward the ceiling and knees bent over your hips.
2. Slowly lower your right arm overhead and extend your left leg down until the left heel lightly taps the floor.
3. Return the arm and leg to the starting position with control.
4. Repeat the movement with the opposite arm and leg, alternating sides.
column19: Maintain controlled movement and proper alignment throughout

column17: Promotes lumbar mobility and gentle posterior chain stretching
column18:
1. Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
2. Draw both knees toward your chest and wrap your hands around your shins.
3. Pull your knees closer to your chest and hold briefly.
4. Lower your feet back to the floor with control to return to the start.
column19: Exhale as you draw both knees in and maintain a long neutral lumbar spine

column17: Improves cervical range of motion and neck mobility
column18:
1. Sit or stand tall with shoulders relaxed and eyes forward.
2. Drop your chin toward your chest and slowly rotate your head to the right in a smooth arc.
3. Continue the motion to look up and then rotate to the left until you complete the circle.
4. Return to neutral and repeat the same slow rotations in the opposite direction.
column19: Keep chin level and rotate slowly within a comfortable range

column17: Enhances thoracic rotation and shoulder mobility while promoting spinal flexibility
column18:
1. Sit sideways on a stable chair with feet flat and hips square.
2. Place your left hand on the chair seat for support and lift your right arm up.
3. Rotate your torso to the left and thread your right arm under your left armpit, sliding it down toward the chair while lowering your right shoulder and head.
4. Reverse the motion to return upright and repeat on the other side.
column19: Keep hips square and thread through with slow controlled motion

column17: Improves shoulder mobility and encourages scapular movement
column18:
1. Sit or stand tall with arms relaxed at your sides.
2. Lift both shoulders toward your ears and roll them forward in a smooth circle.
3. Continue steady forward circles for the desired number of repetitions.
column19: Keep shoulders relaxed and roll slowly maintaining scapular control

column17: Improves finger dexterity and hand coordination
column18:
1. Sit with your forearm resting on a flat surface and your palm facing down.
2. Lift your fingertips and walk them laterally one at a time across the surface.
3. Keep your wrist and forearm stable as each finger moves.
4. Reverse the finger walk to return to the starting position.
column19: Keep wrist neutral and move fingers deliberately without collapsing the palm

column17: Strengthens posterior shoulder and back extensors improving upright posture
column18:
1. Lie face down with your arms extended out to the sides at shoulder height and legs straight.
2. Squeeze your shoulder blades and lift your chest and arms a few inches off the floor.
3. Hold the lifted position while breathing steadily.
4. Lower your chest and arms back down with control.
column19: Squeeze shoulder blades and lift using thoracic extension while keeping neck long

column17: Builds biceps endurance and elbow stabilizer strength
column18:
1. Stand with feet hip-width apart and bend one elbow to 90 degrees.
2. Grasp the working hand’s wrist or palm with the opposite hand to create resistance.
3. Pull upward with the working hand while resisting with the other hand to hold the curl.
4. Maintain steady tension and keep the elbow fixed in place.
column19: Keep elbow tucked by your side and maintain steady tension without swinging

column17: Enhances core stability and coordinated contralateral limb control
column18:
1. Place fists under shoulders and knees under hips in a quadruped position.
2. Engage your core and keep your spine neutral.
3. Extend your right fist forward and left leg back until they are parallel to the floor.
4. Hold briefly, then return to start and repeat on the opposite side.
column19: Keep hips and shoulders square to the floor and extend slowly while bracing the core

column17: Improves scapular retraction and shoulder external rotation mobility
column18:
1. Lie face down with arms extended out to the sides at shoulder height and thumbs pointing up.
2. Keep your neck neutral and legs long on the floor.
3. Lift your chest slightly and squeeze your shoulder blades together to raise your arms a few inches.
4. Hold briefly, then lower with control and repeat.
column19: Keep neck neutral and retract the shoulder blades as you lift arms into a T

column17: Increases shoulder flexion range and thoracic extension
column18:
1. Sit tall with feet flat and hips stacked over knees.
2. Reach both arms overhead with palms facing each other while lengthening through your sides.
3. Lower your arms with control and maintain an upright spine between repetitions.
column19: Sit tall with ribs stacked over hips and reach overhead without arching the lower back

column17: Improves lower body strength and functional transfer ability
column18:
1. Sit near the front of the chair with feet hip-width and flat on the floor.
2. Lean slightly forward and engage your core.
3. Drive through your heels to stand up fully.
4. Slowly lower back down with control until you touch the chair.
column19: Push through your heels and keep your spine neutral

column17: Enhances quadriceps control and knee extension strength
column18:
1. Sit tall on a chair with feet flat and hands resting by your sides.
2. Straighten one knee a few inches until the front thigh tightens.
3. Hold the contraction briefly, then lower the foot back to the floor and repeat.
column19: Extend the knee slowly while keeping the thigh aligned over the knee

column17: Promotes glute activation and hip extension strength for improved posture
column18:
1. Stand sideways to a wall and place one hand on it for balance.
2. Keep your torso upright and squeeze your glute to lift the working leg straight backward.
3. Lower the leg with control and repeat, then switch sides.
column19: Keep your torso upright and hinge from the hip without arching your lower back

column17: Increases ankle dorsiflexion range and foot clearance during walking
column18:
1. Sit on a chair with one foot flat on the floor and the knee bent at about 90 degrees.
2. Pull your toes and foot upward toward your shin using only the ankle muscles.
3. Hold the top position for 2–3 seconds, then lower the foot slowly.
column19: Keep the heel down and pull the toes toward the shin with control

column17: Strengthens intrinsic foot muscles and supports arch stability
column18:
1. Stand barefoot with feet hip-width apart and weight evenly distributed.
2. Keep your toes flat and draw the ball of your foot toward your heel without curling the toes.
3. Hold the shortened arch for 3–5 seconds, then relax.
column19: Shorten the foot by drawing the ball toward the heel without curling the toes

column17: Enhances ankle stability and weight transfer control during standing and walking
column18:
1. Stand tall with feet hip-width apart near a support if needed.
2. Shift your weight onto your heels and lift your toes off the floor, hold for 2–3 seconds.
3. Shift your weight onto the balls of your feet and lift your heels, hold for 2–3 seconds.
4. Return to neutral and repeat the heel-toe holds.
column19: Keep a tall spine and press evenly through heel and toe

column17: Promotes lumbopelvic control and lower back mobility awareness
column18:
1. Stand with your back against a wall and feet hip-width apart a few inches from the base.
2. Tuck your pelvis and press your lower back gently into the wall.
3. Hold the flattened lower back for 3–5 seconds while breathing steadily.
4. Relax the pelvis and repeat the tilt for the desired repetitions.
column19: Tilt the pelvis to press your lower back into the wall while keeping ribs relaxed

column17: Improves spinal mobility and relaxation of trunk muscles
column18:
1. Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
2. Slowly lower both knees to one side while keeping both shoulders on the floor.
3. Hold the gentle twist for 20–30 seconds while breathing evenly.
4. Bring knees back to center and repeat on the opposite side.
column19: Move your knees slowly and keep both shoulders grounded on the floor

column17: Reduces neck muscle tightness and improves neck range of motion
column18:
1. Sit or stand tall with shoulders relaxed and back straight.
2. Place one hand behind your back and tilt your head away from that hand toward the opposite shoulder.
3. Use the other hand to gently increase the lateral stretch at the side of the neck.
4. Hold for 20–30 seconds and then switch sides.
column19: Keep neck long and tilt from the top of the neck without shrugging the shoulder

column17: Enhances thoracic mobility and improves posture alignment
column18:
1. Sit upright on a chair with feet flat and knees at 90 degrees.
2. Place your hands behind your head and align your upper back with the top of the chair back.
3. Slowly lean back over the chair top, keeping your lower ribs down and opening your chest.
4. Hold the gentle extension for a few breaths, then return to upright.
column19: Keep pelvis neutral and hinge from the thoracic spine rather than arching the lower back

column17: Promotes scapular control and supports shoulder stability
column18:
1. Stand facing a wall with feet hip-width apart and hands placed on the wall at shoulder height.
2. Keep arms straight and actively press your shoulder blades down away from your ears.
3. Hold the depressed position for 5–10 seconds, then relax and repeat.
column19: Draw shoulders down away from the ears and focus on scapular depression with control

column17: Improves scapular upward rotation and shoulder mechanics
column18:
1. Stand with your back, head, and hips against a wall and feet a few inches away.
2. Bend elbows to 90 degrees and place forearms and palms flat against the wall in a "W" position.
3. Slowly slide your arms upward along the wall to full extension, keeping contact with the wall.
4. Reverse the motion slowly to the starting position and repeat.
column19: Keep forearms and wrists in contact with the wall and slide using scapular motion only

column17: Strengthens shoulder external rotators and improves joint stability
column18:
1. Stand beside a wall with your elbow bent to 90° and the upper arm snug against your side.
2. Place the outside of your forearm and the back of your hand against the wall with the elbow anchored.
3. Push the back of your hand outward into the wall as if trying to rotate your forearm away from your body.
4. Hold the isometric press with steady breath, then relax.
column19: Tuck elbow to 90 degrees and rotate externally without elevating the shoulder

column17: Enhances forearm rotational mobility and functional wrist control
column18:
1. Sit or stand with your elbow bent to 90° and the upper arm stabilized against your side.
2. Rotate your forearm so your palm turns upward toward the ceiling.
3. Return to the starting palm-down position with control and repeat.
column19: Keep elbow at your side and rotate the forearm slowly with control

column17: Builds core endurance and trains shoulder and wrist stability
column18:
1. Face a wall and place your knuckles on the wall at shoulder height and shoulder-width apart.
2. Step your feet back and extend your body so it forms a straight line from head to heels.
3. Engage your core and keep your spine neutral while pressing through your knuckles.
4. Hold the position with steady breathing, then step forward to stand.
column19: Maintain controlled movement and proper alignment throughout

column17: Opens anterior chest and supports improved breathing mechanics
column18:
1. Stand tall with feet hip-width apart.
2. Clasp your hands behind your back and straighten your arms.
3. Pull your shoulder blades together and lift your chest.
4. Hold the position for 20–30 seconds while breathing evenly.
column19: Squeeze shoulder blades together to open the chest while keeping the neck neutral

column17: Increases latissimus flexibility and enhances overhead reach
column18:
1. Kneel beside a bench with the bench at your side and both knees on the floor.
2. Place the hand nearest the bench on the bench and straighten the arm.
3. Shift your hips away from the bench and lean your torso to the opposite side until you feel a stretch along your side.
4. Hold for 20–30 seconds then switch sides.
column19: Keep hips square and reach through the arm while maintaining a long, aligned spine

column17: Promotes whole body mobility and facilitates muscular tension release
column18:
1. Lie on your back with legs extended and arms resting by your sides.
2. Inhale and reach both arms overhead while pointing your toes away from you.
3. Press your lower back gently into the floor and lengthen from fingertips to toes.
4. Hold for 20–30 seconds while breathing steadily.
column19: Lengthen from fingertips to toes and breathe steadily while keeping the core engaged

column17: Increases quadriceps and gluteal muscle activation for lower limb strength
column18:
1. Sit upright on a chair with knees bent and feet flat against a wall or solid surface.
2. Lean back slightly and press both feet into the wall as if trying to move it.
3. Squeeze your thigh muscles and hold the pressing effort steady.
4. Release the pressure slowly and relax your legs.
column19: Keep spine neutral and press evenly through both heels

column17: Strengthens hip abductor muscles to improve lateral hip stability and control
column18:
1. Lie on your side with legs straight and stacked and your head supported.
2. Keep your hips aligned and toes pointing forward.
3. Lift the top leg upward slowly until you feel the outer hip engage.
4. Lower the leg with control back to the starting position.
column19: Anchor your lower back to the mat and lift through the hip without rotating

column17: Enhances hip flexor strength and thigh muscle coordination
column18:
1. Lie on your side with the bottom leg bent for support and the top leg straight.
2. Bring the top knee forward and upward to flex the hip while keeping the torso still.
3. Return the top leg straight back down in a controlled motion.
column19: Keep hips stacked and core engaged to prevent rolling

column17: Improves hip flexor length and hip extension mobility
column18:
1. Kneel on one knee with the other foot flat in front.
2. Tuck your pelvis and shift weight forward to feel a stretch in the front of the hip.
3. Keep your torso upright and squeeze the glute of the kneeling leg.
4. Hold for your chosen time, then switch sides.
column19: Maintain an upright torso and gently tuck the pelvis during the stretch

column17: Builds calf muscle strength and ankle plantarflexion power
column18:
1. Sit with legs extended and loop a resistance band around the ball of one foot.
2. Hold the band ends at your hips and press the ball of the foot away by pointing the toes.
3. Slowly return the foot toward neutral while controlling the resistance.
4. Complete the desired reps, then switch to the other foot.
column19: Control the ankle motion and avoid bouncing through the range

column17: Enhances balance and ankle proprioception on unstable surfaces
column18:
1. Stand on a soft surface and lift one foot slightly off the ground.
2. Keep hips level and a tall posture while focusing on a fixed point.
3. Maintain balance for the chosen duration while breathing steadily.
4. Lower the foot and repeat on the other side.
column19: Keep a slight bend in the standing knee and maintain a level pelvis

column17: Increases toe and forefoot mobility and intrinsic muscle activation
column18:
1. Sit with one leg extended and the ankle relaxed.
2. Grasp the toes and gently pull them upward to extend the toes.
3. Hold the stretch for a few seconds while keeping the foot relaxed.
4. Release and repeat, then switch sides.
column19: Stabilize the ankle and lift the toes deliberately without compensating elsewhere

column17: Promotes gait precision and improves foot placement coordination
column18:
1. Place a strip of tape on the floor to form a straight line.
2. Stand with one foot directly in front of the other at the start of the line.
3. Walk heel-to-toe along the tape while keeping eyes forward and arms out for balance.
column19: Walk slowly heel-to-toe with eyes forward and controlled posture

column17: Encourages thoracic extension and improves upper back mobility
column18:
1. Lie face down with hands under shoulders and elbows close to the body.
2. Press through your hands to straighten the arms and lift the chest while keeping hips on the floor.
3. Lift only as far as comfortable while keeping the neck neutral.
4. Lower back down with control and repeat.
column19: Keep the hips grounded and extend the thoracic spine with slow controlled movement

column17: Improves chest and triceps strength while enhancing pressing stability
column18:
1. Lie on your back on the floor with knees bent and a dumbbell in each hand at chest level.
2. Press the dumbbells straight up until your arms are nearly locked.
3. Slowly lower the dumbbells until your elbows lightly touch the floor.
4. Pause briefly and press the weights back up to the starting position.
column19: Retract shoulder blades and keep ribs down while pressing with controlled tempo

column17: Strengthens back muscles and improves posterior chain stability
column18:
1. Stand with feet hip-width, hold a dumbbell in each hand, and hinge at the hips until your torso is nearly parallel to the floor.
2. Keep your back flat and your core braced.
3. Pull the dumbbells toward your ribcage, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
4. Lower the dumbbells with control back to the hanging position.
column19: Hinge from hips, keep spine neutral, pull elbows toward hips with control

column17: Builds shoulder strength and supports overhead pressing control
column18:
1. Sit upright on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height and feet flat on the floor.
2. Brace your core and keep your back neutral.
3. Press the dumbbells overhead until your arms are nearly extended.
4. Lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height with control.
column19: Sit tall, brace core, press overhead tracking wrists over elbows

column17: Increases biceps strength and enhances elbow flexion control
column18:
1. Stand with feet hip-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with palms facing forward.
2. Keep elbows close to your torso and curl the dumbbells up toward your shoulders.
3. Squeeze your biceps at the top, then lower the dumbbells slowly to the starting position.
column19: Keep elbows pinned to sides and avoid swinging the torso

column17: Develops triceps strength and improves elbow extension stability
column18:
1. Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and hold one dumbbell vertically with both hands overhead.
2. Keep upper arms close to your head and bend your elbows to lower the dumbbell behind your head.
3. Extend your elbows to raise the dumbbell back overhead until your arms are nearly straight.
column19: Keep elbows close to ears, hinge slightly and exhale as you extend

column17: Improves shoulder abductor strength and lateral shoulder stability
column18:
1. Stand with feet hip-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with palms facing inward.
2. With a slight bend in the elbows, lift the dumbbells out to the sides until they reach shoulder height.
3. Pause briefly at the top, then lower the dumbbells slowly back to your sides.
column19: Lift with controlled motion keeping a slight elbow bend and scapula stable

column17: Strengthens anterior deltoids and improves forward shoulder control
column18:
1. Stand feet hip-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand at your thighs with palms facing your body.
2. Keep a slight bend in your elbows and exhale as you raise the dumbbells straight forward to shoulder height.
3. Pause briefly at shoulder height with wrists neutral and shoulders down.
4. Inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly back to the starting position.
column19: Lead with the elbow keeping shoulders down and core braced

column17: Enhances upper trapezius strength and improves shoulder elevation stability
column18:
1. Stand feet hip-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with palms facing your body.
2. Keep your arms straight and exhale as you lift your shoulders straight up toward your ears.
3. Pause and squeeze your traps at the top without rolling your shoulders.
4. Inhale and lower your shoulders back down under control.

column17: Activates posterior shoulder muscles to improve scapular stability and posture
column18:
